articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: Giant inflatable match ball breaks loose; Messi, Mbappé, Haaland set for action
articles.description: World Cup group-stage days rarely get better than this. Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland are all in action. Follow live updates with ESPN.

articles.headline: World Cup live updates: Senegal keeping France at arm's length
articles.description: Join us for all the updates as France kick off their 2026 World Cup campaign against Senegal in New Jersey.

images.name: France [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Loic VENANCE / AFP via Getty Images)

articles.headline: Ghana vs. Panama - kick-off time, team news, how to watch Black Stars' FIFA World Cup opener
articles.description: Ghana will kick off their FIFA World Cup campaign on Wednesday in Toronto, with a Group L clash against Panama.

images.name: Antoine Semenyo [1296x729]
images.caption: Antoine Semenyo is the key to Ghana's success at this FIFA World Cup, with the Manchester City striker arguably the best player in the English Premier League this year.

images.name: Carlos Queiroz: 'Everything is possible' for Ghana ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Carlos Queiroz: 'Everything is possible' for Ghana ahead of World Cup

articles.headline: Could Thomas Partey's World Cup absence be a blessing in disguise for Ghana?
articles.description: When Carlos Queiroz named Thomas Partey in Ghana's FIFA World Cup squad, the logic was clear... as were the risks. Will his absence against Panama prove to be a blessing, in retrospect?

images.name: Thomas Partey [1296x729] - Copy (2)
images.caption: Thomas Partey's absence might be an opportunity rather than a setback for Ghana.
images.alt: Thomas Partey of Ghana  during the international friendly match between Wales and Ghana at Cardiff City Stadium on June 02, 2026 in Cardiff, Wales

images.name: Jordan Ayew [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Jordan Ayew's performance will be particularly key in the absence of Thomas Partey.

images.name: Mark Ogden takes driverless taxi to U.S. vs. Paraguay

articles.headline: Former South Africa stars urge Hugo Broos to change course at FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hugo Broos is popular in South Africa, but words from former Bafana players suggest faith is being tested.

images.name: Teko Modise [1296x729]
images.caption: Teko Modise is one of several Bafana Bafana players who have urged Hugo Broos to take a different approach when Bafana Bafana face Czechia.
images.credit: RODRIGO ARANGUA / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Laurens: South Africa 'need to improve massively' to advance
images.caption: Laurens: South Africa 'need to improve massively' to advance.

articles.headline: Neymar trains alone at Brazil World Cup camp after calf injury
articles.description: Neymar, who has been out for a month with a right calf injury, trained on the pitch sidelines on Tuesday for the first time since arriving in the United States.
